title: OAuth Scopes and Permissions Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of OAuth 2.0 scopes and how permissions are managed in modern applications.

questions.question: What is the primary function of an OAuth Scope?
options: To make the database faster.
To define an application's role on the server.
To limit an application's access to a user's account with their consent.
To encrypt the final bundle size.

questions.question: What is the 'Principle of Least Privilege' in OAuth context?
options: Only give users one role.
Request the absolute minimum number of scopes necessary for your application to function.
Don't allow users to see any data.
Make the application as low-cost as possible.

questions.question: Who must provide explicit consent for the requested scopes?
options: The developer of the application.
The owner of the API.
The user being authenticated.
The server administrator.

questions.question: What does a Resource Server (API) typically do with a token's scopes?
options: It ignores them and allows all access.
It verifies them to ensures the requested action is permitted.
It automatically generates a backup of the data.
It shares the scopes with other applications.

questions.question: What happens if a user revokes one of the scopes through their identity provider settings?
options: The application will continue to work normally.
The server will gracefully handle 'Insufficient Scope' errors.
The application will immediately crash.
None of the above.
